judgements.reason: GGUF signed cards + SSTI scans thesis (target Q3 2026) is directly reinforced by today's SGLang CVE-2026-5760 patch — supply-chain risk vector explicitly cited as 'malicious GGUF distributed via model repositories'. Reasoning.because precondition holds. VR is older (last on 2026-04-28) but directly relevant news re-energizes the thesis.
No retracted bridges. Today's SGLang CVE patch story (jinja2 sandboxing fix; supply-chain GGUF vector named) is the strongest support bridge in recent memory for this thesis.
Hugging Face, MITRE, CERT/CC, CSAI actors all remain active. No Need flipped to done — signed-card mandate has not yet shipped. SGLang patch is precedent, not delivery.

8 chain edges + 8 relation edges added 2026-05-05 (linkages with .7db9cba2, .4c31ca30, .5094f95c, .678c96be, .c08481a6, .995feb03, .4bd551bf). All structural; one 'entails' edge to .995feb03 strengthens the chain logic, doesn't break it.
